payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

Deliver weekly earnings transparency to children

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Claiming earned pocket money through completed task verification
payload.justification: SG904 describes the core strategic process of enabling children to claim earned pocket money, representing fundamental enterprise capability.

payload.description: Establishing task-based compensation structure
payload.justification: SG905 identifies the parental process of establishing compensation structure, a strategic enterprise function for defining earning rules.

payload.description: Tracking child progress toward earning opportunities
payload.justification: SG906 articulates progress tracking as a strategic process enabling child visibility, representing ongoing enterprise monitoring function.

payload.description: Providing weekly earnings transparency to children
payload.justification: SG907 specifies the strategic process of delivering earnings transparency on a weekly basis, representing core enterprise reporting capability.

payload.description: Generating oversight reporting for disbursement decisions
payload.justification: SG908 defines the strategic process of providing parental oversight reporting to enable disbursement decisions, representing executive decision-support function.

payload.description: Pocket money as strategic enterprise compensation asset
payload.justification: SG904 and SG908 both identify pocket money as a core thing of strategic importance—the fundamental compensation unit that flows through the system based on task completion and parental disbursement decisions.

payload.description: Task as unit of compensable activity
payload.justification: SG904 references completed tasks as the mechanism for earning, and SG905 describes task-based compensation structure, establishing tasks as strategic entities of importance.

payload.description: Earning opportunity as strategic visibility artifact
payload.justification: SG906 explicitly identifies available earning opportunities as things requiring visibility, indicating strategic importance as discoverable entities in the enterprise.

payload.description: Weekly earnings as temporal financial aggregation
payload.justification: SG907 identifies weekly earnings as a distinct strategic entity requiring transparency, representing a time-bounded aggregation of compensable work.

payload.description: Task completion determines pocket money eligibility
payload.justification: SG904 establishes strategic relationship between completed tasks and the ability to claim earned pocket money, representing core business logic at planner level.

payload.description: Progress tracking links child stakeholders to earning opportunities
payload.justification: SG906 articulates the strategic connection between children and their ability to track progress toward earning opportunities, representing key enterprise relationship.

payload.description: Weekly earnings reporting and transparency cycle
payload.justification: SG907 explicitly specifies weekly earnings transparency as a business objective, establishing a recurring strategic cycle important to enterprise operations.

payload.description: Task completion triggering pocket money claim eligibility
payload.justification: SG904 identifies completed tasks as the enabling event for claiming earned pocket money, representing strategically significant state change.

payload.description: Disbursement decision event following oversight review
payload.justification: SG908 describes oversight reporting enabling disbursement decisions, implying a strategic decision event triggered by reporting completion.

payload.description: Child as earning and claiming stakeholder
payload.justification: Multiple signals (SG904, SG906, SG907) identify children as strategic actors who claim pocket money, view opportunities, track progress, and receive earnings transparency.

payload.description: Parent as compensation structure authority and disbursement decision-maker
payload.justification: SG905 and SG908 identify parents as strategic stakeholders with authority to establish compensation structure and make disbursement decisions based on oversight reporting.

payload.description: Enable children to claim earned pocket money
payload.justification: SG904 explicitly expresses this as the core strategic purpose of the enterprise, representing fundamental goal.

payload.description: Establish task-based compensation for children
payload.justification: SG905 identifies parental motivation to create task-based compensation structure as strategic goal driving enterprise design.

payload.description: Provide children with visibility into earning opportunities, progress, and weekly earnings transparency
payload.justification: SG906 articulates stakeholder goal for visibility and tracking, representing strategic objective for child engagement and transparency. | SG907 specifies this as a business objective, representing strategic goal for financial transparency and child awareness.

payload.description: Enable informed parental disbursement decisions through oversight
payload.justification: SG908 defines parental need for oversight reporting to enable disbursement decisions, representing strategic goal for governance and control.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Youth Compensation Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for establishing, structuring, and governing the task-based compensation framework through which children earn and claim pocket money, including the fundamental units of compensable activity and financial assets.

payload.name: Earning Opportunity Transparency
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to providing visibility and transparency into available earning opportunities, individual progress toward earnings, and temporal financial aggregations that inform both youth participants and parental oversight.

payload.name: Claim Entitlement Governance
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility for determining and validating eligibility to claim earned compensation based on verified task completion, establishing when entitlement arises and how it may be exercised.

payload.name: Parental Disbursement Authority
payload.description: Enterprise governance structure defining parental authority over compensation structure design and disbursement decisions, supported by oversight reporting to enable informed financial stewardship.

payload.name: Youth Stakeholder Participation
payload.description: Enterprise recognition of children as active earning and claiming stakeholders with defined participation rights and responsibilities within the compensation framework.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish pocket money as a strategic compensation asset.
payload.rationale: Defines pocket money as an enterprise asset category at the strategic scope level, establishing its fundamental role in the compensation framework.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise tasks as the unit of compensable activity.
payload.rationale: Establishes the fundamental structural entity that forms the basis of the compensation framework.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall govern task-based compensation eligibility based on task completion.
payload.rationale: Establishes the governance rule linking task completion to compensation entitlement, derived from the relationship CCI and the goal of establishing task-based compensation for children.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a task-based compensation structure.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to create and maintain the overall compensation framework, derived from the How-column process CCI.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility into available earning opportunities.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to transparency regarding earning opportunities as strategic visibility artifacts, fulfilling the goal of providing children visibility into opportunities.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish weekly earnings as a temporal financial aggregation.
payload.rationale: Defines the enterprise-level structural entity of weekly earnings as a temporal financial construct, establishing what the enterprise recognizes as a financial aggregation period.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain progress tracking that links child stakeholders to earning opportunities.
payload.rationale: Enterprise obligation to maintain the relationship between children and opportunities through progress tracking, supporting the visibility goal.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide weekly earnings transparency to children.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to deliver weekly financial transparency within the established reporting cycle, fulfilling the core transparency goal.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim earned pocket money.
payload.rationale: Why-column CCI expressing enterprise commitment to enable a specific entitlement capability at scope level.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish entitlement to claim pocket money upon verified task completion.
payload.rationale: How-column and When-column CCIs combined express a single enterprise obligation: the act of establishing claim entitlement based on the triggering event of verified task completion.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish parental authority over compensation structure design and disbursement decisions.
payload.rationale: Defines the enterprise governance structure placing parental role as the authoritative decision-maker for compensation and disbursement matters.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide oversight reporting to enable informed parental disbursement decisions.
payload.rationale: Combines the oversight reporting capability (How) with its purpose of enabling informed decisions (Why) into a single enterprise commitment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall govern disbursement decisions following oversight review.
payload.rationale: Establishes the governance rule that disbursement decisions occur only after oversight review, constraining the sequence of enterprise activities.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise children as stakeholders with the capacity to earn and claim compensation.
payload.rationale: Establishes the foundational enterprise commitment to recognise children as active participants in the compensation framework, carrying both earning and claiming rights. This is a governance constraint that defines the scope of legitimate stakeholders.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Requirement elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the core purpose of enabling children to claim earned pocket money through completed tasks.
Identifies parental motivation to establish task-based compensation structure for children.
Articulates stakeholder goal for child visibility into available earning opportunities and progress tracking.
Specifies business objective of providing weekly earnings transparency to children.
Defines parental stakeholder need for oversight reporting to enable pocket money disbursement decisions.
